headlines.title: Trump says US considering 'winding down' Iran war; Natanz nuclear facility attacked
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump announced the U.S. is considering winding down military operations against Iran as the war enters its fourth week. Iranian media reported that the Natanz nuclear enrichment facility was attacked by U.S.-Israeli forces. Trump stated the U.S. is close to meeting its objectives but insisted other nations should take the lead in policing the Strait of Hormuz. Over 2,000 people have been killed in Iran since the conflict began on February 28.

headlines.title: US deploys additional 2,500 Marines and warships to Middle East amid Iran war escalation
headlines.summary: The U.S. announced deployment of three more amphibious assault ships and approximately 2,500 additional Marines to the Middle East region. This follows the redirection of another 2,500 Marines from the Pacific. The deployments come despite Trump's comments about winding down operations, creating mixed messages about U.S. strategy. More than 50,000 U.S. troops are now in the region.

headlines.title: Iran threatens attacks on tourist sites worldwide as war expands
headlines.summary: Iran's top military spokesperson warned that 'parks, recreational areas and tourist destinations' worldwide will not be safe for the country's enemies. The threat comes three weeks into the escalating war and raises concerns that Tehran may revert to using militant attacks beyond the Middle East. Iran also fired two ballistic missiles toward the U.S.-British military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean.

headlines.title: Oil prices surge 50% due to Iran war, hobbling global economy
headlines.summary: Brent crude oil has soared to around $108 per barrel, up from roughly $70 before the war began. The Strait of Hormuz, conduit for around a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas supplies, has been effectively closed to most shipping. Energy price shocks are fueling inflation and hitting consumers and businesses hard, creating a major political liability for Trump ahead of November elections.

headlines.title: Israel launches 'broad wave of strikes' on Beirut targeting Hezbollah
headlines.summary: Israel began a wave of strikes targeting Hezbollah militants in Beirut's southern suburbs. Israeli strikes in Lebanon have displaced more than 1 million people, with over 1,000 killed according to the Lebanese government. Israel also attacked Tehran, Karaj, and Isfahan, while Iranian missiles triggered air raid sirens across Israel.

headlines.title: Saudi Arabia downs 20 drones in eastern oil region
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia reported downing 20 drones in just a couple of hours in the country's eastern region, which is home to major oil installations. The defense ministry confirmed there were no injuries or damage. The attacks came as Iran escalates strikes on Gulf neighbors after Israel bombed Iran's South Pars offshore natural gas field.

headlines.title: Trump lifts sanctions on Iranian oil shipments to ease price surge
headlines.summary: The Trump administration announced it will lift sanctions on Iranian oil loaded on ships, a move aimed at wrangling soaring fuel prices. The pause applies to oil loaded as of Friday and is set to end April 19. The move follows a similar 30-day pause on certain Russian oil shipments as the administration seeks ways to boost global oil supplies during the war.

headlines.title: European gas prices surge 35% after Israel hits Iran's biggest gas field
headlines.summary: Natural gas prices in Europe surged as much as 35% this week after Israel struck Iran's largest gas field and Iran responded by attacking neighbors' energy infrastructure. The European Union has urged members to lower gas-storage targets and start refilling reserves gradually to curb demand.

headlines.title: India and Japan negotiate safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: India has persuaded Iran to let some of its ships through the Strait of Hormuz, with liquefied petroleum gas tankers leaving last week. Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iran's President on Saturday. Iran's Foreign Minister also indicated readiness to let Japanese vessels pass through the strait, which carries around 90% of Japan's oil imports.

top_story.title: Trump says US considering 'winding down' Iran war; Natanz nuclear facility attacked
top_story.summary: President Donald Trump announced the U.S. is considering winding down military operations against Iran as the war enters its fourth week. Iranian media reported that the Natanz nuclear enrichment facility was attacked by U.S.-Israeli forces. Trump stated the U.S. is close to meeting its objectives but insisted other nations should take the lead in policing the Strait of Hormuz. Over 2,000 people have been killed in Iran since the conflict began on February 28.
